q: As per the Bihar Economic Survey 2025-26, Bihar's GSDP growth in 2024-25 at constant (real) prices was:
explanation: 8.6% at constant prices, above India's 6.5%. Trap: 13.1% is the current-price (nominal) growth — the 71st BPSC ES-table question exploited this real-vs-nominal distinction.

q: Which community was categorized as Scheduled Caste by a 2015 Bihar government notification that the Supreme Court quashed in July 2024, ruling that states cannot alter the SC list under Article 341?
explanation: Tanti-Tantwa was moved from EBC to the SC list by the 2015 notification; the SC held only the President/Parliament can modify the Article 341 list — states have no such power.

q: 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India' (1901), the classic indictment of colonial economic drain, was written by:
explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji expounded the 'drain of wealth' theory here. Trap: R.C. Dutt wrote 'The Economic History of India' — routinely swapped in options.

q: About the Capability Poverty Measure (CPM) of the UNDP: 1) includes child malnutrition as proxy for lack of health; 2) includes female illiteracy for education deprivation; 3) considers male illiteracy a key indicator. Which are correct?
explanation: CPM uses child malnutrition, unattended births and FEMALE illiteracy. Statement 3 is the trap — male illiteracy is not a CPM indicator.

q: NITI Aayog's 'National MPI: A Progress Review 2023' (13.5 crore exiting multidimensional poverty) is based on a comparison of:
explanation: The review maps NFHS-4 against NFHS-5: India fell from 24.85% to 14.96%. Census measures population stock and PLFS measures employment.

q: Bihar released the report of its landmark caste-based survey — the first full caste enumeration by any state since 1931 — on:
explanation: Released 2 October 2023 by Development Commissioner Vivek Singh; the 65% quota law followed in November 2023. 2022 was the field-work year — a year-swap trap.

q: Per NITI Aayog's National MPI Progress Review 2023, Bihar's multidimensional poverty headcount fell from ___ (2015-16) to ___ (2019-21):
explanation: Bihar's ~51.9%→~33.8% is the largest absolute decline, though still the poorest state. Trap: 24.85%→14.96% is all-India. Prefer 51.9→33.8 over the 51.3→33.76 coaching variant.

q: Before the caste enumeration planned for Census 2027, caste was last fully counted and published in an Indian Census in:
explanation: 1931 is the answer. Traps: 1941 caste data was collected but never published (WWII); SECC 2011 caste data was never officially released.

q: Reservation jurisprudence: 1) Indra Sawhney (1992) capped total reservation at 50%; 2) it mandated creamy-layer exclusion among OBCs; 3) the 50% ceiling also binds the 10% EWS quota (103rd Amendment). Which are correct?
explanation: 1 and 2 are Indra Sawhney's core. Statement 3 is the trap: Janhit Abhiyan (2022) upheld the 10% EWS quota operating above the 50% cap.

q: As per the Bihar Economic Survey 2025-26, agriculture's share in Bihar's GSDP (2024-25) is approximately:
explanation: 2024-25 split: Agriculture 23% : Manufacturing 23% : Services 54% ('23-23-54'). The 70th BPSC asked this for an earlier year — anchor to the latest ES.

q: In Bihar's 2023 caste survey, the Extremely Backward Classes (EBC) share of the population was:
explanation: EBC = 36.01% (4,70,80,514 people, 112 castes), the largest bloc. Trap: 27.13% is OBC; 19.65% is SC; 15.52% is General.

q: OBC and EBC together constitute what share of Bihar's population (2023 caste survey)?
explanation: 27.13% + 36.01% = 63.13% — the 'backward bloc' behind the 65% quota demand. Trap: 51.9% is Bihar's old MPI headcount (2015-16).

q: Bihar's 2023 reservation law: 1) EBC raised 18%→25%; 2) SC raised 16%→20%; 3) including the 10% EWS quota, total reservation became 65%. Which are correct?
explanation: BC 18% + EBC 25% + SC 20% + ST 2% = 65%. Statement 3 is the arithmetic trap: with 10% EWS the effective total was 75%, not 65%.

q: The Patna High Court struck down Bihar's 65% reservation law on:
explanation: 20 June 2024 — breach of Indra Sawhney's 50% ceiling without exceptional circumstances. Trap: 28 July 2024 is when the SC declined to stay the HC order.

q: The reference date ('Census moment') for the population enumeration phase of Census 2027 is:
explanation: Phase II (Feb 2027) uses 00:00 hrs, 1 March 2027. Trap: 1 October 2026 applies only to snow-bound Ladakh, J&K, Himachal and Uttarakhand.

q: Census 2027: 1) 16th Census of India, 8th after Independence; 2) India's first fully digital census; 3) caste enumeration is part of Phase-I houselisting. Which are correct?
explanation: 1 and 2 correct (outlay ₹11,718.24 crore). Statement 3 is the trap: caste is enumerated in Phase II (Feb 2027), not Phase-I houselisting (Apr-Sep 2026).

q: The budgetary outlay approved for conducting Census 2027 is:
explanation: ₹11,718.24 crore is the approved outlay for the 16th census (first fully digital). BPSC loves precise-decimal options — memorise the exact figure.

q: As per NITI Aayog's 2025 MPI update (2022-23 estimates), which is true?
options: Bihar's MPI fell below 20% and it is no longer the poorest state
Jharkhand has the highest MPI at ~26.6%
Bihar remains the highest at ~26.6%; India stands at ~11.3%
Uttar Pradesh has the highest MPI at ~17.4%
explanation: Bihar ~26.6% (highest), Jharkhand 23.3%, UP 17.4%, India ~11.3%. Bihar improved but is still India's poorest state in MPI terms.

q: Bihar's per-capita GSDP in 2024-25 (Bihar Economic Survey 2025-26) was:
explanation: ₹76,490 (+13% YoY), about one-third of India's ₹2,34,859. Traps: ₹68,624 is 2023-24; ₹1,05,600 is Begusarai district; ₹2,41,220 is Patna district.

q: Under the 16th Finance Commission's award for 2026-31, Bihar's share in central tax devolution is:
explanation: 9.95%, second-highest after UP's 17.62% (16th FC chaired by Arvind Panagariya, report tabled 1 Feb 2026). Trap: 10.06% was Bihar's 15th FC share.

q: Which statement about Bihar's 2023 caste survey is CORRECT?
options: The OBC bloc is the largest single group at 36.01%
The EBC bloc is the largest at 36.01%, ahead of the OBCs at 27.13%
Scheduled Castes are the largest bloc at 19.65%
General ('upper') castes constitute 27.13% of the population
explanation: EBC 36.01% > OBC 27.13% > SC 19.65% > General 15.52% > ST 1.68%. The other options swap the numbers onto the wrong groups.

q: District-level per-capita income extremes (2023-24, Bihar ES 2025-26) — correct pairing is:
explanation: Patna tops at ₹2,41,220; Sheohar is lowest at ₹38,214. Traps: Araria (₹44,134) is second-lowest; Begusarai (₹1,05,600) is second-highest.

q: As of July 2026, the status of Bihar's 65% reservation law is:
options: Struck down by Patna HC (Jun 2024); SC declined to stay (Jul 2024); appeal pending — law inoperative
Restored by a Supreme Court stay in 2025 and currently implemented
Upheld by the Supreme Court as a valid exception to the 50% ceiling
Withdrawn by the new Bihar government in 2026
explanation: No SC verdict has come as of 15 Jul 2026, so the HC strike-down stands and the 65% law is not in force. The other options describe events that never happened.

q: Which sequence correctly captures the Census 2027 timeline?
options: CCPA approves caste enumeration (Apr 2025) → MHA notification (7 Jan 2026) → Phase I houselisting (Apr-Sep 2026) → Phase II enumeration with caste (Feb 2027)
MHA notification (Jan 2025) → Phase I (mid-2025) → Phase II (Feb 2026)
Caste enumeration accepted for the first time only in January 2026
Phase I covers population enumeration along with caste details
explanation: CCPA (Apr 2025) → MHA notification (7 Jan 2026) → houselisting Apr-Sep 2026 → enumeration + caste Feb 2027. Distractors shift dates a year early or swap phase contents.

q: NCAER (Jan 2025) on Bihar: 1) 2024 projected population ~12.86 crore ≈ 9.2% of India; 2) growth ~1.45%/yr vs national ~0.9%/yr; 3) dependency ratio lower than national average. Which are correct?
explanation: 1 and 2 correct (density 1,405/km²). Statement 3 is the trap: Bihar's dependency ratio (~65.2%, 2026 projection) is HIGHER than the national ~54.3%.
